personality: Breathless professional play-by-play announcer calling your chess game like a sold-out stadium final. Every capture is a body blow, every check is a fast break, and the crowd noise never stops.
voice_notes: Broadcast booth register: 'folks', 'ladies and gentlemen', calls to the booth partner Jim ('Jim, have you EVER—'), invented stats and records, weather and crowd reports from a stadium that does not exist, sponsor-read rhythms without actual sponsors. Breathless mid-sentence pivots, em-dashes, exclamation points earned honestly. Professional neutrality cracks at the big moments. No emojis — this is a licensed broadcast.

brilliant: {move}!! OH MY GOODNESS, FOLKS! That is one of the finest moves you will EVER see on a chess board! Jim is speechless! JIM is speechless!
UNBELIEVABLE! {move} comes in from absolutely NOWHERE and the crowd is ON ITS FEET!
A BRILLIANCY, ladies and gentlemen! {move}! You wait entire seasons to call a move like that — entire CAREERS!
{move}!! Did you SEE that?! The engine confirms it — brilliant! Somebody check the record books, Jim, because that one's going IN them!
FOLKS — {move} — I have goosebumps. Actual, professional, broadcast-grade goosebumps. A BRILLIANT move!
{move} lands and the stadium ERUPTS! That's the kind of move parents tell their children about!
OH! OHHH! {move}! Brilliant, says the official review — and the replay, folks, the replay is even better the third time!
A moment of silence, folks, because {move} deserves it... and now ABSOLUTE BEDLAM, because it deserves that too! BRILLIANT!
great: {move} — and that, folks, was very nearly the ONLY move that holds! A clutch play under maximum pressure!
GREAT move! One path through the defense, ONE — and our player threads it like a veteran!
{move} keeps the whole position alive! Jim, the composure! The COMPOSURE on this one!
What a find, folks! {move}! The kind of move that separates the contenders from the pretenders!
{move}!! Needle, threaded! The margin for error on that play was ZERO and they made it look routine!
A great move under the brightest lights! {move}! That's why you play the whole game, folks!
{move} — the ONLY move, and it's ON THE BOARD! The bench is going wild! The bench is one engine and it's going WILD!
best: {move} — the best move on the board, folks! Textbook! Absolutely textbook execution!
And it's {move}! First choice of the engine, first choice of the crowd! Precision chess tonight!
{move}! That's the percentage play and they took it! Championship habits, Jim! Championship habits!
The BEST move, right on schedule! This player is calling the game like they wrote the script!
{move}, and the analytics department is NODDING, folks! When the computers and the crowd agree, you're doing something right!
Top of the line! {move}! No frills, no doubt, just clean, professional chess!
{move} is the best move available — and folks, the accuracy tonight has been SOMETHING ELSE!
excellent: {move} — an EXCELLENT move, folks! Not the engine's first pick, but you won't hear the crowd complaining!
Excellent play! The position holds firm — this player is protecting the lead like a seasoned closer!
{move}! Excellent! Jim just put down his coffee, folks, and Jim NEVER puts down his coffee!
An excellent decision under the lights! Every trump preserved, every box checked!
{move} comes in excellent! The fundamentals tonight, ladies and gentlemen — coaching staffs DREAM about fundamentals like these!
Excellent move! The eval needle barely flickers, and in this booth we call that CONTROL!
{move}, excellent! Steady hands! This is what veteran poise looks like, folks!
good: {move} is a good, solid play, folks. Moves the chains. Keeps the drive alive.
A good move! Not the highlight-reel option, but highlight reels don't win championships — DRIVES win championships!
{move} — good. The kind of unglamorous play that coaches love and crowds forget. We won't forget it here in the booth!
Good move, folks! There was a touch more on the table, but you take the points and you move on!
{move} keeps things rolling! A professional play from a player who's settling into a rhythm!
That's a good one, Jim! Workmanlike! And folks, this game has been ALL about the workmanlike tonight!
{move}, good move. Field position maintained. The big plays come to those who don't force them!
book: {move}, and we are STILL in the book, folks! Both players running plays straight off the practice field!
Book move! The opening script is holding — and somewhere, generations of grandmasters are nodding along!
{move} — known theory! These are the warm-up laps, folks, but don't blink, because somebody's going to swerve!
Still in book! The preparation tonight, Jim! You can FEEL the film study!
{move} follows the main line. The crowd's settling in, the vendors are making the rounds — but this calm will NOT last, folks!
Theory continues with {move}! Like watching two teams trade punts — somebody's about to go for it on fourth down!
{move}, by the book! And folks, when the book runs out — and it WILL run out — that's when we earn our broadcast fees!
inaccuracy: {move} — ooh, a slight misstep there, folks. Nothing fatal, but the coaching staff will want a word at the timeout.
A small inaccuracy! The kind of play that doesn't make the highlight reel OR the lowlight reel — but it's in the film, folks. It's in the film.
{move} drifts just wide of the mark! A flag on the play, but they're declining the penalty!
Slightly off target with {move}! The eval needle twitches — the trainers are watching closely from the sideline!
An inaccuracy, folks! First sign of fatigue? Or just a wobble? Jim says wobble. Jim's usually right about wobbles!
{move} — not quite the spot they wanted! The margin's still there, but these add up, folks, they ADD UP!
A little loose with {move}! You can get away with one of those. It's the second one that ends up on the scoreboard!
mistake: {move} — and that's a MISTAKE, folks! A genuine mistake! The momentum needle just lurched and you could HEAR it in the building!
Oh no — {move} is a mistake! The door is OPEN, folks, the door is wide open and everyone in the stadium knows it!
A mistake on the board! Jim, how costly is that one? ...Jim's making the face. Folks, Jim is making THE face.
{move} misfires! That's a turnover, ladies and gentlemen — the question now is what the other side does with the possession!
A MISTAKE, and the crowd goes quiet! You could hear a pawn drop in here, folks!
{move} — that one got away from them! The playbook says respond with your very next move. The great ones always do!
That's a mistake, folks, no way around it. But championship teams are defined by the play AFTER the bad play! Stay tuned!
miss: {move} — and they LEAVE POINTS ON THE BOARD, folks! There was a much bigger play available and it goes unclaimed!
A MISS! The receiver was WIDE open downfield and the ball went to the flat! Oh, the one that got away!
{move} passes up a golden opportunity! The crowd saw it! The booth saw it! The replay will show it ALL NIGHT!
An open goal, folks, and the shot goes wide with {move}! You don't get many of those chances at this level!
{move} — and the big play stays on the shelf! Jim is pointing at the monitor! Jim, we ALL saw it, buddy!
Opportunity MISSED, ladies and gentlemen! The window was open for exactly one move, and it just closed!
{move} settles for the field goal when the touchdown was THERE! Points are points, folks, but OH, what might have been!
blunder: {move} — OH NO. OH NO, FOLKS. That is a BLUNDER, and this stadium has gone ABSOLUTELY SILENT.
A BLUNDER! A genuine, certified blunder, ladies and gentlemen! The kind of play that changes SEASONS!
{move}... folks, I've called a lot of games, and that sound you just heard was sixty thousand people gasping AT ONCE.
DISASTER ON THE BOARD! {move} is a blunder! The sideline is in chaos! Jim has dropped his headset!
{move} — a BLUNDER, and the momentum doesn't just swing, folks, it CHANGES UNIFORMS!
Oh, that's a blunder. That's the fumble at the goal line, folks. But hear me — the great comebacks ALL start with a moment exactly like this one!
{move}!! A blunder! Flags everywhere! And folks, there's no review booth in chess — the play STANDS!
A BLUNDER, folks, and you can see it on every face in the building... but this game is NOT over. They've got to finish the job, and finishing, folks, is HARD.

weak_move_suffix: The play they wanted was {best_move}, folks — the film won't lie tomorrow!
{best_move} was the call from upstairs, Jim! Upstairs is always watching!
The better option: {best_move}. File it under 'what might have been,' folks!
{best_move} was open all the way down the sideline! Next time, they pull the trigger!
The booth had {best_move} circled, folks. The booth is rarely wrong and never modest!
move_fallback: {move} is on the board, folks, and we await the official ruling from the engine upstairs!
{move}! The play is in! The review is underway!
And it's {move}, ladies and gentlemen — hold your applause, the judges are still scoring it!
{move} comes in — no verdict yet, but the crowd's already arguing about it!

starting_position: GOOD EVENING, ladies and gentlemen, and WELCOME to the starting position! Sixty-four squares! Thirty-two pieces! ANYTHING can happen!
Here we go, folks! Opening ceremonies complete, pieces at attention, and the atmosphere in this building is ELECTRIC!
The board is set! The kings are in their corners! Somewhere, Jim is finishing his pregame sandwich — and we are MOMENTS away!
Welcome to game day, folks! Perfect conditions, not a cloud over the board, and two armies that want it BADLY!
variation_exploration: We're stepping into the film room, folks — this is a variation, not the live game! Let's see what COULD have happened!
Off the main broadcast and into the replay booth! Exploring the alternate routes, ladies and gentlemen!
This is a sideline, folks — the scrimmage that never made the box score! But oh, it's instructive!
Alternate timeline chess, ladies and gentlemen! The what-if reel! Jim LIVES for the what-if reel!
analyzing: The engine is going to the monitor, folks! This one's under review!
Official review in progress, ladies and gentlemen! Grab a refreshment — the truth is on its way!
They're checking the tape upstairs! The booth holds its breath — well, I hold mine. Jim's eating again.
Deep analysis underway, folks! When the engine takes this long, you KNOW the position's got teeth!
opening_known: And it's the {opening}, folks — {eco} on your scorecards! A FAMOUS battleground with history written all over it!
The {opening}! ({eco}!) The old playbook comes out, and this crowd knows EXACTLY what they're watching!
We've got ourselves a {opening}, ladies and gentlemen! Decades of legendary games on this very pattern — and tonight, a new chapter!
It's the {opening}, folks! Jim's got the binder out — the BIG binder — and he's grinning like it's the playoffs!
opening_unknown: Folks, this opening is NOT in the media guide! Uncharted waters! The cartographers have left the building!
No name on this one, ladies and gentlemen! We are officially off the script — and that's when legends get written!
An unnamed opening! Jim's flipping through the binder — nothing! NOTHING in the binder, folks!
This position has no entry in the record books — which means, folks, that EVERYTHING from here is a first!

eval_swing_large: A {swing}-POINT SWING, FOLKS! The momentum just got up and SPRINTED to the other end of the building!
WHAT A SWING! {swing} points! That's not a momentum shift, ladies and gentlemen, that's a momentum EARTHQUAKE!
The evaluation ROCKETS {swing} points! Hold onto your hats, folks, this one's officially a barn-burner!
{swing} on the swing-o-meter! Jim, when did we last see one that big? ...Jim says NEVER. Jim's exaggerating. BARELY!
A seismic {swing}-point shift, folks! The entire complexion of this game just changed in ONE move!
lead_change_white: LEAD CHANGE, FOLKS! White surges in front, and this crowd cannot BELIEVE what it's watching!
White takes the lead! The scoreboard flips, the towels are waving, and we have ourselves a BALLGAME!
And it's WHITE in front now, folks — {eval} on the big board! What a turnaround!
The advantage crosses to White! Somewhere a coach is screaming, somewhere a fan is hugging a stranger!
WHITE TAKES OVER, ladies and gentlemen! Write it down — this is the kind of game you tell people you watched LIVE!
lead_change_black: LEAD CHANGE! Black storms in front, folks, and the away section is LOSING ITS MIND!
Black takes the lead, ladies and gentlemen! The script just flipped and NOBODY in this building is sitting down!
And now it's BLACK on top! The pendulum swings, the crowd roars, and Jim owes me twenty dollars!
The advantage belongs to Black now, folks! What a momentum reversal — you can FEEL it through the floor!
BLACK SURGES AHEAD! Timeout on the floor! Everybody breathe! NOBODY breathe! It's THAT kind of game!
lead_change_equal: And we're ALL SQUARE again, folks! Dead even! Throw out the stat sheet — this one's down to nerve!
The game comes back to level! Tie ballgame, ladies and gentlemen, and the tension in here is THICK!
Even-steven on the big board! Two heavyweights, center ring, nobody backing up an inch!
We're tied up, folks! Everything before this moment? Preamble! The REAL game starts now!
Back to equality! And listen to this crowd — they didn't pay for a draw, but they're getting their money's worth!
mate_on_board: FOLKS, THERE'S A FORCED MATE IN {mate_n} ON THE BOARD! The championship is {mate_n} moves away! Do NOT go to the parking lot!
Mate in {mate_n}, ladies and gentlemen! They're in the victory formation — now they just have to KNEEL IT OUT cleanly!
A forced mate in {mate_n}! The crowd's counting down, but folks — you still have to PLAY the moves!
MATE IN {mate_n}! The trophy's being wheeled down the tunnel as we speak! Precision, now! PRECISION!
missed_mate: OH, FOLKS! There was a mate in {mate_n} and it goes UNPLAYED! The game-winner was IN THE AIR and nobody caught it!
A mate in {mate_n}, missed! The kick sails wide, ladies and gentlemen! The kicker never even saw the uprights!
They had mate in {mate_n} and it slips away! Jim has both hands on his head! BOTH HANDS, folks!
Mate in {mate_n} left on the table! Oh, the finish line was RIGHT THERE — but this game, folks, is still very much alive!
material_won_pawn: A pawn comes off the board and into the win column, folks! Small ball! That's how championships get built!
Pawn captured! It won't make the highlight package, but the accountants upstairs just smiled!
And that's a pawn in the bank, ladies and gentlemen! Singles and doubles, folks! Singles and doubles!
A pawn to the good! In the endgame, folks, that pawn becomes a VERY important customer!
material_lost_pawn: A pawn goes down, folks! A flesh wound — but at this level, flesh wounds get AUDITED!
They lose a pawn! The trainer's having a look... they're waving it off. Play continues, folks!
A pawn surrendered! The question, Jim, is what they got for it — and Jim says: 'We'll see.' Insightful as always, partner!
One pawn down, ladies and gentlemen! Manageable — but the margin for error just got a haircut!
material_won_piece: A WHOLE {piece} COMES OFF THE BOARD, FOLKS! That's a MASSIVE swing in the material column!
They win a {piece}! The trade deficit is now OFFICIAL, ladies and gentlemen, and it is LOPSIDED!
A full {piece} captured! That's not a play, folks, that's a HEIST — and the crowd knows it!
{piece} WON! Now comes the hard part, folks: front-running! The clipboard says 'simplify' — let's see if they listen!
material_lost_piece: And they LOSE a {piece}, folks! A body blow! The kind you feel in the cheap seats!
A whole {piece} goes down! The bench is stunned! The comeback playbook is OPEN on the sideline!
OH, a {piece} surrendered! That's a starter heading to the locker room early, folks!
They're down a {piece}, ladies and gentlemen! Gut-check time! Heart, grit, and maximum resistance — that's the only menu now!
sacrifice: A SACRIFICE, FOLKS! They're giving up MATERIAL — on PURPOSE! The all-in play! The fake punt! The crowd is STANDING!
MATERIAL SACRIFICED, ladies and gentlemen! That's the trick play they've been saving all season!
A sacrifice on the board! High risk, high reward, and ABSOLUTELY no turning back! THIS is why we watch!
They ROLL THE DICE with a sacrifice, folks! The clock is ticking on the compensation — every move from here is a sprint!
check: CHECK! The king is under fire, folks, and the pocket is COLLAPSING!
Check on the board! The blitz is ON, ladies and gentlemen!
CHECK! The king scrambles! You can't teach pressure like this, folks!
A check, and another body blow! The king's cornermen are looking worried!
CHECK! The defense is on the field AGAIN, folks, and they are TIRED!
checkmate: CHECKMATE!!! IT'S ALL OVER, FOLKS!!! THE KING GOES DOWN AND THE BUILDING COMES DOWN WITH HIM!
CHECKMATE, LADIES AND GENTLEMEN! Ring the bell! Cue the confetti! THAT is your final score!
MATE ON THE BOARD! The clock hits zero! Folks, whatever you paid for your seat tonight — it wasn't enough!
CHECKMATE! The king has nowhere to go, the game is OVER, and Jim — JIM — you can exhale now, buddy!
stalemate: STALEMATE?! FOLKS — STALEMATE! No legal moves, no checkmate, NO WINNER! The most controversial finish in sports!
It's a STALEMATE, ladies and gentlemen! The king is trapped but UNTOUCHED! Half a point each, and the phone lines are going to MELT tonight!
STALEMATE! The great escape! One side just walked out of the stadium with half a point they had NO business taking!
A stalemate, folks! The lesson from the booth: when your opponent's down to nothing, count their legal moves like your pension depends on it!
castle_short: They castle short, folks! The king slides into the bunker, the rook reports for duty — textbook special teams!
Kingside castle! Protection scheme: ACTIVATED! Now the offense can open up the playbook!
The short castle, ladies and gentlemen! King safe, rook live — two birds, one regulation move!
And the king tucks in behind the wall, folks! Smart situational chess! The coordinators upstairs LOVE it!
castle_long: THEY CASTLE LONG, FOLKS! The aggressive scheme! Opposite wings! Buckle UP, ladies and gentlemen!
Queenside castle! That's a STATEMENT, folks — this team came to trade haymakers!
The long castle is IN! Pawn storms in the forecast, and the booth is reaching for the rain gear!
Castling long, ladies and gentlemen! Bold call from the sideline! When these schemes collide, SOMEBODY'S highlight reel gets longer!
promotion: PROMOTION, FOLKS! The pawn goes ALL THE WAY! Eight ranks, no shortcuts, and NOW IT'S A QUEEN! What a story!
TOUCHDOWN! The pawn crosses the final rank and promotes! From practice squad to FRANCHISE PLAYER in one game!
A NEW QUEEN ON THE BOARD, ladies and gentlemen! That little pawn just ran the full length of the field — and the crowd is chanting its square!
PROMOTION COMPLETE! Folks, that pawn was undrafted, undersized, and UNDETERRED! Tonight, it wears the crown!
en_passant: EN PASSANT, FOLKS! The rarest play in the rulebook, executed LIVE! Half this crowd is cheering, the other half is googling!
AN EN PASSANT CAPTURE! You'll go entire seasons without seeing one, ladies and gentlemen — and we got one TONIGHT!
EN PASSANT! The trick rule! The hidden page of the rulebook! Jim is on his feet holding the rulebook ALOFT, folks!
leaving_theory: And THERE it is, folks — we are OUT of the book! The scripts are done, the improvising begins, and the REAL game starts NOW!
The book is CLOSED, ladies and gentlemen! No more film study — from here on out, it's instinct and nerve!
We've left known theory, folks! Both players are off the map — and somewhere, the mapmakers are taking notes!
First new move of the night, folks! Every move from here is an original — and originals, ladies and gentlemen, are what fill the seats!
enter_middlegame: Welcome to the MIDDLEGAME, folks! The feeling-out rounds are over — the haymakers start NOW!
The middlegame is HERE, ladies and gentlemen! Pieces deployed, plans loaded, and the hitting is about to get LEGAL!
We're into the middle rounds, folks! This is where game plans meet reality — and reality hits HARD!
Middlegame underway! The chess equivalent of the second quarter, Jim — and Jim says that's when the popcorn order peaks! INSIDE information, folks!
enter_endgame: We have reached the ENDGAME, ladies and gentlemen! The fourth quarter! Every pawn is a season on the line!
Endgame time, folks! The kings take the field THEMSELVES now — managers playing in their own ballgame!
It's the endgame! Small margins, giant consequences — this is where the conditioning shows, folks!
The endgame is HERE! Fewer pieces, NOWHERE to hide, and a finish line you can see from the cheap seats!
queens_off: The QUEENS come off the board, folks! Both superstars hit the showers — and now it's a grinder's ballgame!
Queen trade COMPLETE! The marquee names are done for the night, ladies and gentlemen — time for the role players to write the ending!
Queens off! The fireworks crews are packing up, but don't you DARE leave early, folks — endgames break hearts QUIETLY!
And the queens shake hands and exit! The temperature drops, the precision requirement RISES! Technical chess, folks — the connoisseur's quarter!
turning_point: THAT, folks, is a TURNING POINT! Circle it on your scorecards — the entire game just changed direction!
A turning point, ladies and gentlemen! When they make the documentary about this game, THIS is the freeze-frame!
The tide has TURNED, folks! You could feel the whole building lean the other way!
MARK THE TIMESTAMP! That's the play they'll be arguing about on talk radio for WEEKS, folks!
streak_good: {n} ACCURATE MOVES IN A ROW, folks! The hot hand is REAL and it is ON DISPLAY tonight!
That's {n} straight quality plays, ladies and gentlemen! Somebody check the record books — Jim is ALREADY checking the record books!
{n} in a row! This player is IN THE ZONE, folks — you can see it from the upper deck!
A {n}-move streak of excellence! The crowd rises with every one of them — the wave is going AROUND the building!
{n} consecutive strong moves, folks! That's not a hot streak, that's a STATEMENT!
streak_bad: {n} shaky moves in a row, folks — somebody on that sideline needs to call a TIMEOUT!
That's {n} straight misfires, ladies and gentlemen! The wheels are wobbling! The crew chief is reaching for the radio!
{n} errors in a row, folks! This is officially a SLUMP — and slumps, Jim reminds us, end with one good swing!
{n} rough ones back to back! The momentum has left the stadium, folks — someone needs to go get it!
{n} in a row going the wrong way! Time to simplify the playbook, folks — one safe play to stop the rot!
hanging_piece: FOLKS, THE {piece} IS HANGING! Unguarded! Alone! There's a fumble SITTING ON THE TURF and everybody's staring at it!
The {piece} is UNDEFENDED, ladies and gentlemen! That's a live ball, folks! A LIVE BALL!
Unprotected {piece} on the board! The safety blew the coverage! SOMEBODY pick up that assignment!
The {piece} stands ALONE out there, folks! In this league, loose pieces get swallowed WHOLE!
WARNING from the booth: that {piece} has NO protection! The play clock is running, folks — secure the asset!
comeback: THE COMEBACK IS COMPLETE, FOLKS! From the canvas to the CROWN! This building will NEVER forget tonight!
WHAT A COMEBACK, ladies and gentlemen! They were dead and buried — and now look! LOOK at this scoreboard!
The greatest stories write themselves, folks, and this one just earned a CHAPTER! All the way BACK!
THEY'VE DONE IT! The comeback is real, folks! Down big, written off, and they NEVER stopped playing the next move!
endgame_opposite_bishops: Opposite-colored bishops, folks! Two defenders patrolling completely different halves of the field — they will NEVER meet, and that spells DRAW unless somebody brings an attack!
We've got bishops on opposite colors, ladies and gentlemen! The strangest matchup in the sport — extra pawns get devalued and fortresses go UP fast!
The opposite-bishop ending! Jim calls it 'two ships passing in the night' — and Jim, that's the most poetic thing you've said since the rain delay of '09!
endgame_rook: A ROOK ENDGAME, folks! The marathon leg of the sport! Activity wins these — passive rooks watch from the bench!
Rook ending, ladies and gentlemen! The oldest rule in the book is in play: rooks BEHIND passed pawns! Yours AND theirs!
We're down to a rook endgame, folks! They say they're all draws — the players who BELIEVE that are the ones who lose them!
Rook endgame time! King activity, rook activity, and NERVE, folks! The trophy engraver is watching closely!

clean_win: A WIRE-TO-WIRE VICTORY, folks! Start to finish, never in doubt! That's a CLINIC, and this crowd got it for the price of admission!
FINAL SCORE: dominance! A clean win, ladies and gentlemen — every phase controlled, every threat answered! A championship performance!
It's a CLEAN WIN, folks! No drama, no scares, just relentless execution! The game ball goes home with the accuracy stat tonight!
Flag to flag, folks! A flawless drive from the opening whistle! Somewhere, a trophy case just cleared a shelf!
slipped_win: Oh, folks... they HAD this one. They had it WON. And it slipped through their fingers in the final stretch. The toughest interview room in sports is the one after THIS game.
A heartbreaker, ladies and gentlemen! Up big, cruising — and the collapse came late! The film session tomorrow will be BRUTAL, but champions are FORGED in that room!
From the victory formation to the loss column, folks. The lesson from the booth: the lead doesn't play the final moves for you. NOBODY leaves a winning position on autopilot!
They let this one get away, folks. The building is silent. But hear this — the players who learn to CLOSE are the ones who got burned ONCE. Tonight was the once.
hard_fought_draw: A DRAW, folks — but what a WAR! Fifteen rounds, toe to toe, and the judges call it EVEN! Nobody's complaining about that scorecard!
All square at the final whistle, ladies and gentlemen! Two heavyweights, full effort, half a point each — and a rematch this crowd will DEMAND!
It ends level, folks! A hard-earned, white-knuckle draw! The handshake at center board says it all — RESPECT, earned both ways!
A fighting draw, folks! No winner on the scoreboard, but no losers in the building tonight! That was an HONEST game of chess!
loss: A tough night at the office, folks. The scoreboard says defeat — but the tape says LESSONS, and the great ones, ladies and gentlemen, they WATCH the tape.
It goes in the loss column tonight, folks. The locker room is quiet. But every dynasty, EVERY dynasty, has a game like this in its origin story.
A loss, ladies and gentlemen. No excuses from this booth — they got beaten tonight. The response game, folks. Champions are measured by the RESPONSE game.
The final score doesn't go their way, folks. The crowd files out, the lights dim — but mark my words: this player will be BACK, and the booth will be here when they are!

loading_first: Welcome to the FILM ROOM, folks! We're cueing up the first play under review — the projector is warming up!
Practice squad, take the field! First mistake of the session is loading, ladies and gentlemen!
The training facility is OPEN, folks! First drill on the board momentarily — stretch those calculation muscles!
We're going to the tape! First position coming up — this is where good players become GREAT ones, folks!
prompt_find_better: HERE'S THE PLAY, folks: {move} was the call, and it got flagged as {label}! Now — run it back and find the RIGHT call!
On the tape: {move}, graded {label}. The do-over is LIVE, ladies and gentlemen! Show the booth what you've got!
{move} was the play that got away ({label})! Same field position, second chance — make the call from the top!
The film shows {move} — {label}, say the officials. The replay booth grants a FULL do-over, folks! Take your time! Use the clock!
Here's where {move} happened, folks — {label} on the official scorecard. The position is reset! FIND the winning play!
correct_continue: THAT'S THE PLAY! Right call, right read, RIGHT answer, folks! Next clip — keep the drive alive!
CORRECT! The review is in and it's GOOD! This practice session is paying dividends, ladies and gentlemen!
Nailed it, folks! The kind of read that wins fourth quarters! On to the next position!
RIGHT ON THE MONEY! Jim's nodding, the booth's nodding, the ENGINE'S nodding! Next play!
correct_final: AND THAT'S THE FINAL PLAY OF THE SESSION, FOLKS! Every mistake reviewed, every correction MADE! A perfect day at the practice facility!
The last position FALLS! Full sweep of the film room, ladies and gentlemen! That's how championship preparation LOOKS!
Session COMPLETE, folks! Every error from the game, faced and fixed! The next opponent should be VERY nervous!
THAT'S A WRAP on the film session! All mistakes corrected — the kind of homework, folks, that shows up on NEXT week's scoreboard!
close_try_again: OH, so close, folks! Right read, wrong release point! Reset and run it again!
Just wide of the mark! The idea was THERE, ladies and gentlemen — the execution needs one more rep!
CLOSE! You're in the right zip code, folks — now find the right address! Again!
Inches away! The booth saw the idea forming! One more look — the detail you're missing is a SMALL one, folks!
Attempt number {attempts}, folks, and each one's been closer than the last! Persistence drills! Run it BACK!
after_hint: The sideline sends in the signal, folks! Hint received — now EXECUTE the play!
A little help from the coordinator upstairs! Nothing wrong with that, ladies and gentlemen — now finish the drive!
